channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Notícias - Partido Novo
item.description: Romeu Zema, Governor of Minas Gerais and pre-candidate for president by the Novo Party, filed an impeachment request against Alexandre de Moraes, Supreme Court minister. The action was motivated by revelations of messages between Moraes and banker Daniel Vorcaro, exposing alleged abuses by the STF in the Master Case.

item.title: Segurança de Lula abateu mais de 135 mil drones: como é equipamento usado?
item.description: Lula's security neutralized over 135 thousand drones in the last four years, according to GSI/PR data. The blocks occurred at presidential events and around official buildings in Brasilia.

item.title: Com investimentos 65% abaixo da meta, programa de Lula para PCDs empaca
item.description: The federal Novo Viver sem Limites program, from the Lula government, completes two years with low adherence from states and municipalities and investments 65% below the target. The initiative for the inclusion of people with disabilities faces implementation difficulties and lack of resources.

item.title: Campanha de Flávio dá ultimato para Ratinho Jr desistir de candidatura ŕ Presidęncia
item.description: Governor Ratinho Jr. rejected PL's proposal to withdraw from the presidential race and support Flávio Bolsonaro, maintaining his intention to be chosen by PSD. The PL threatens to break the alliance in Paraná and support another gubernatorial candidate, possibly Sergio Moro or Alexandre Curi, to secure a platform for Flávio in the state.

item.title: Campanha de Flávio dá ultimato para Ratinho Jr desistir de candidatura à Presidência
item.description: Paraná governor Ratinho Jr. met with Rogério Marinho and received a proposal to withdraw from the presidential race in favor of Flávio Bolsonaro, with a possible vice-presidential spot. Ratinho Jr. rejected the proposal.

item.title: Governo Lula vê nova onda de influência de apoiadores de Bolsonaro na gestão Trump para classificar facções como terroristas
item.description: The Lula government detected renewed influence from Bolsonaro supporters within the Trump administration to classify Brazilian criminal factions as terrorists. This classification could enable US military interventions in Brazil and violate national sovereignty. Brazilian diplomats see this as an alliance between Trumpist radicals and Bolsonaro allies to create political problems for the government in an election year.

item.title: Tarcísio diz que EUA declararem PCC como terrorista é “oportunidade”
item.description: São Paulo Governor Tarcísio de Freitas called the potential U.S. designation of Brazilian criminal factions like PCC and CV as terrorist organizations an 'opportunity,' saying it would facilitate intelligence cooperation and financial resources. The Brazilian government fears this classification could enable U.S. military interventions, a stance that contrasts with Tarcísio's view and the Lula administration's previous position that criminal factions cannot be considered terrorists as they don't defend causes or ideologies.

item.title: CPI para investigar Moraes e Toffoli é ilegal, diz Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro claims the CPI to investigate STF ministers Alexandre de Moraes and Dias Toffoli is illegal, calling author Alessandro Vieira a hypocrite. He signed the CPI request but says it's invalid and accuses Vieira of political maneuvering.

item.title: José Manuel Restrepo: “Si buscan votos, se equivocaron de candidato”
item.description: José Manuel Restrepo, former Finance Minister and rector of EIA University, accepted to be the vice-presidential candidate on Abelardo de la Espriella's far-right ticket, despite previously stating he was an economist, not a politician. The rapid change of position demonstrates the accelerated and surprising nature of this electoral process.

item.title: Centrão mapeia votos no STF para tentar soltar Daniel Vorcaro e evitar delação
item.description: Centrão politicians are mobilizing to try to secure the release of banker Daniel Vorcaro in the STF's Second Panel, fearing he might make a plea bargain if he remains in prison. They are mapping votes and operating behind the scenes to build a majority favorable to his release, taking advantage of Minister Dias Toffoli's recusal from the case. The strategy aims to prevent Vorcaro from revealing political and financial relations in a possible collaboration.

item.title: Senador leva garrafa de whisky ao plenário em protesto contra STF
item.description: Senator Cleitinho brought a bottle of Macallan whisky to the Senate floor to protest against STF ministers, referencing a 2024 London event funded by Banco Master that cost about R$ 3.2 million. The senator called for impeachment of ministers Dias Toffoli and Alexandre de Moraes, claiming the money spent was not honest and represents an affront to the population.

item.title: Durante seu programa, Ratinho faz comentário transfóbico contra deputada Érika Hilton
item.description: TV host Ratinho made transphobic comments while criticizing the election of deputy Erika Hilton as president of the Women's Commission. He stated that 'to be a woman you have to be a woman' and questioned whether she was 'deputy or deputy', denying Hilton's gender identity.

item.title: Datafolha: Descrédito de instituições cresce, e 52% desconfiam de partidos políticos
item.description: Datafolha survey shows 52% of Brazilians distrust political parties, reflecting growing institutional discredit. Between December 2024 and March 2026, public trust or assessment worsened for 7 out of 8 institutions surveyed.

item.title: TSE julga caso que pode criar jurisprudência sobre desvio de verbas de cotas raciais na eleição
item.description: The TSE is analyzing a case that could establish jurisprudence on the diversion of electoral funds intended for black candidates to white candidates. The case may set a precedent on the proper use of racial quotas in campaign financing.

item.title: Com renda declarada de R$ 570 milhões em 2024, Vorcaro recebeu restituição do Imposto de Renda
item.description: Daniel Vorcaro, owner of Banco Master, declared R$ 570 million in income for 2024 and received a tax refund of R$ 28,764.97. His personal wealth grew 87% in one year, mainly from selling company shares, while Master faced financial troubles and investigations.

item.title: Violência doméstica: Senado aprova tornozeleira para agressor e monitoramento com uso de IA
item.description: The Federal Senate approved a project to create an AI monitoring system for aggressors using electronic ankle bracelets. The system will alert victims via cell phone if the aggressor violates the protective measure and approaches. The project goes to the Chamber of Deputies and provides for a national app with an emergency button and AI risk analysis.

item.title: Ipsos-Ipec: governo Lula tem pior avaliação em controle de gastos e combate à inflação; melhor resultado é em educação
item.description: Ipsos-Ipec poll shows Lula government's worst ratings are in spending control (51% bad/awful) and inflation fight (50% bad/awful). Best ratings are in education (36% good/excellent) and hunger/poverty fight (35% good/excellent), though negative ratings still prevail in all areas.

item.title: STF começa a julgar anistia a partidos que descumpriram cotas de mulheres e negros
item.description: The STF began judging an action that questions a constitutional change that amnestied parties that did not meet quotas for resources for women's and black people's campaigns in elections before 2022. Rede Sustentabilidade party and Fenaq allege that the amendment violates constitutional principles of racial and gender equality by preventing sanctions against parties and allowing the use of resources in future elections.

item.title: BRB cobra na Justiça dívida de R$ 799 mil de ex-presidente investigado no caso Master
item.description: Banco de Brasília (BRB) filed a lawsuit to collect a R$ 799,000 debt from former president Paulo Henrique Costa, who is under investigation by the STF for suspected irregularities in transactions between BRB and Banco Master. The bank is seeking to seize assets or garnish 30% of the former executive's salary to pay off the debt, which includes unpaid loans since December 2023.

item.title: É #FAKE foto de Vorcaro ao lado de Flávio Bolsonaro e 'Careca do INSS'; imagem foi criada com IA
item.description: A fake image allegedly showing Daniel Vorcaro, Flávio Bolsonaro, and the 'Careca do INSS' was created using artificial intelligence. The photo circulating on social media was debunked by verification tools that identified AI watermarks.

item.title: CPMI do INSS: em decisão, Dino diz que presidente do Palmeiras pode optar por comparecer à sessão desta quinta ou marcar nova data
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Flávio Dino allowed Palmeiras and Crefisa president Leila Pereira to choose between attending the INSS CPI session this Thursday or setting a new date for testimony, suspending for now the possibility of coercive conduct. The defense cited short notice and a previous Dino decision on global voting for privacy breaches, which was not accepted.

item.title: Deputados e familiares prestam homenagem a Marielle na Câmara e citam condenação 'histórica' de mandantes dos assassinatos
item.description: Parliamentarians and relatives of Marielle Franco and Anderson Gomes remembered at the Chamber the historic conviction of the crime masterminds in 2018. The solemn session highlighted Marielle's legacy and the importance of holding public agents involved with militias accountable. Relatives emphasized the need to ensure spaces of power for women and the importance of the conviction for society.

item.title: Violência é citada por 27% como maior preocupação no Brasil, diz Quaest; corrupção aparece em seguida
item.description: Quaest poll shows 27% of Brazilians cite violence as the country's main concern, followed by corruption at 20%. The survey interviewed 2,004 people between March 5-9, with a 2-point margin of error.

item.title: Quaest 1º turno: Lula lidera em dois cenários e tem empate técnico com Flávio Bolsonaro em outros cinco
item.description: Quaest poll shows Lula leading Flávio Bolsonaro in two first-round scenarios, with up to 7 percentage points advantage. In five other scenarios, they are technically tied with just 1 point difference. Among other pre-candidates, Ratinho Júnior stands out with 7% and Ronaldo Caiado with 4%.

item.title: O que Valdemar planeja após encontro com Eduardo Bolsonaro nos EUA
item.description: Valdemar Costa Neto met with Eduardo Bolsonaro in the US to define PL's electoral strategies, including candidacies in São Paulo. They discussed party unity around amnesty for Bolsonaro and January 8th prisoners, while negotiating Senate seats in São Paulo.

item.title: TSE tem 2 a 0 para cassar Castro e Bacellar; Nunes Marques pede vista
item.description: The TSE postponed the trial on Rio Governor Claudio Castro's impeachment after Minister Ferreira voted for his conviction. Nunes Marques requested a review, postponing the decision to March 24. Castro may lose his mandate and become ineligible for 8 years if convicted of political and economic abuse of power.

item.title: Senado aprova criação de cargos no Executivo; impacto é de R$ 5,3 bi
item.description: The Senate approved a bill creating over 24,000 new permanent positions in the federal public service, with an estimated budgetary impact of R$5.3 billion in 2026. The proposal includes hiring 3,800 new professors for higher education and over 9,500 for federal institutes of education, science and technology, as well as establishing direct elections for university rectors.

item.title: TSE tem 2 votos para condenar Cláudio Castro à inelegibilidade; Nunes Marques suspende julgamento
item.description: The TSE formed two votes to annul the mandate of Rio de Janeiro governor Cláudio Castro and state deputy Rodrigo Bacellar, declaring them ineligible for alleged irregular hiring of electoral workers. The trial was suspended by Minister Kassio Nunes Marques' request for review and is set to resume on March 24. The actions involve suspicions of abuse of power and misuse of public resources through state institutions like Ceperj and Uerj.

item.title: Kassio pede vista e adia por 15 dias decisão sobre futuro político de Castro
item.description: Minister Kassio Nunes Marques requested a 15-day extension to analyze the case that could make Rio governor Cláudio Castro ineligible. The TSE vote was tied 2-2 when suspended, with resumption scheduled for March 25. The case involves accusations of irregular temporary hiring in 2022 allegedly used for electoral purposes.

item.title: TSE tem 2 votos a 0 pela cassação de Cláudio Castro, mas Kassio interrompe julgamento
item.description: The TSE recorded two votes for the impeachment of Rio de Janeiro Governor Claudio Castro for political and economic abuse in the 2022 elections. The trial was interrupted after Minister Kassio Nunes Marques requested a review. Five votes remain to be cast in the next session scheduled for March 24.

item.title: TSE tem 2 votos a 0 pela cassação de Cláudio Castro, mas Kassio interrompe julgamento
item.description: The TSE registered two votes for the annulment of Rio de Janeiro's governor, Cláudio Castro, for political and economic abuse in the 2022 elections, but the trial was interrupted by Kassio Nunes Marques.

item.title: Homens engajados pelo fim da violência contra as mulheres terão reconhecimento do Senado
item.description: The Senate approved a project creating the White Ribbon Medal to annually recognize up to three men or institutions that develop relevant actions against violence against women. The honor will be preferably awarded during the week of December 6th, marking the National Day of Men's Mobilization to End Violence Against Women.

item.title: Senado aprova criação do Instituto Federal do Sertão Paraibano; projeto segue para sanção
item.description: The Senate approved the creation of the Federal Institute of Sertão Paraibano, which will be headquartered in Patos and split from the Federal Institute of Paraíba. The bill, authored by the Executive Branch, also restructures careers in the Executive Branch and creates positions in various agencies. The proposal, led by House Speaker Hugo Motta, now goes to presidential sanction.

item.title: Nunes Marques pede vista e adia julgamento de cassação de mandato e inelegibilidade de Cláudio Castro no TSE
item.description: Minister Nunes Marques requested additional time at the TSE, postponing until March 24th the judgment on the removal of Rio governor Cláudio Castro's mandate and his ineligibility. Minister Cármen Lúcia voted for removal, supported by Antonio Ferreira, in a case involving accusations of political abuse and irregularities in Ceperj and Uerj management.

item.title: Deputados lançam carta-compromisso para incluir a proteção animal no debate eleitoral
item.description: Deputies launched an animal protection commitment letter during a solemn session at the Chamber of Deputies, aiming to include the topic in the 2026 electoral debate. The initiative seeks to strengthen animal rights defense policies in public power, despite resistance in Parliament and the existence of about 185 thousand abandoned animals in the country.

item.title: TSE tem dois votos para condenar Cláudio Castro à inelegibilidade
item.description: TSE minister votes to convict Cláudio Castro for political-economic abuse in temporary hiring that allegedly benefited 2022 campaign. Governor faces risk of ineligibility that could affect Senate bid plans, depending on final process outcome.

item.title: Senado aprova Programa Antes que Aconteça, que previne violência de gênero
item.description: The Senate approved the Antes que Aconteça Program to prevent gender violence and assist abused women. The initiative includes integrated action by government branches, AI monitoring of aggressors, humanized spaces in police stations, and training for public agents.

item.title: Áudio: Senado aprova reestruturação de carreiras do Executivo; texto segue para sanção
item.description: The Senate approved an executive project to restructure public sector careers, adjust salaries for some categories, and strengthen university autonomy. The proposal also values professionals working in border regions and expands technical education, now moving to presidential sanction.

item.title: YouTube expande proteção contra deepfakes para políticos
item.description: YouTube expanded its deepfake detection technology to politicians, journalists, and government officials, enabling removal of fake content simulating their appearance without consent. The AI-powered tool identifies manipulated videos, audios, and images, but removals will be individually evaluated to preserve public interest content like parodies. Participants must undergo identity verification, and collected data will only be used for security purposes, not to train AI models.

item.title: Davi parabeniza TV Senado por documentário exibido na Tela Quente
item.description: Senate President Davi Alcolumbre praised TV Senado's documentary 'Quando elas se movimentam', broadcast on Globo and BBB, highlighting women's rights. He also announced the inauguration of the Sala Lilás, a pioneering space in legislative houses for supporting violence victims.

item.title: Ministério Público junto ao TCU pede afastamento do presidente do IBGE, Marcio Pochmann
item.description: The prosecutor from the Ministry Public alongside TCU requested the removal of IBGE's president, Marcio Pochmann, due to alleged irregularities in the management of the institution. The main criticisms include replacing career civil servants with newly hired staff in technical positions and the attempt to create the IBGE+ Foundation, which could exceed the legal attributions of the institute.

item.title: Pesquisa aponta que 51% desaprovam Lula e 43% aprovam; avaliação ótima e boa cresce 3 pontos
item.description: Ipsos/Ipec poll shows 51% disapprove of Lula's administration and 43% approve. Excellent and good ratings increased by 3 percentage points compared to December. Survey interviewed 2,000 voters in 131 municipalities with a 2 percentage point margin of error.

item.title: Câmara aprova realização de campanhas educativas permanentes contra a violência doméstica
item.description: The Chamber of Deputies approved a bill establishing general rules for permanent information and education campaigns against domestic and family violence against women. The proposal requires all federative entities to promote informative campaigns across various communication media, addressing all forms of violence described in the Maria da Penha Law. The text also provides for the participation of civil society organizations and periodic evaluations of campaign effectiveness.

item.title: Vazamentos do caso Master acirram choque entre STF, Congresso e Polícia Federal
item.description: Leaks from the Master Bank case intensified tensions between the Supreme Court, Congress, and Federal Police, with ministers criticizing the exposure of private conversations. Dispute over information origin involves CPMI and PF, while investigation continues with analysis of seized cell phones.

item.title: “Embaixador oficial do narcotráfico”, diz Caiado sobre Lula
item.description: Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado harshly criticized President Lula, calling him the 'official ambassador of drug trafficking and criminal factions in Brazil'. The statement came in response to a report about Brazilian government concerns over the possible designation of PCC as a terrorist organization by the US.

item.title: Senado aprova reestruturação de carreiras federais com 13 mil novos professores
item.description: The Senate approved a bill restructuring federal careers, creating over 24,000 new permanent positions, including 3,800 university professors and 9,500 for federal institutes. The proposal also establishes direct election of rectors and benefits approximately 270,000 public servants. The text now goes to presidential sanction.
